title: Alert on SLO burn rate
description: Page on multi-window error-budget burn rate rather than a raw threshold so alerts track real risk.
body: Use when alerting on reliability against an SLO. Do not page on a single static error-rate threshold — it fires on harmless blips and misses slow burns. Actions: define the SLI, set the SLO target and error budget, and alert on burn rate over multiple windows (fast burn on a short window, slow burn on a long one) so both acute and gradual exhaustion page appropriately. Validation signals include the alert firing for genuine budget-threatening burn and staying quiet for tolerable noise. Risk boundary: a misconfigured burn-rate alert that never fires hides budget exhaustion — verify it against a simulated burn before trusting it.
